name: Marshallese
notes: Marshallese (Kajin M̧ajeļ), a Micronesian language of the Marshall Islands. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. The consonant letters of the modern (MOD) orthography map cleanly onto the palatalised / velarised / rounded consonant series (⟨p b j t k kw m ṃ n ṇ ñ l ḷ r d rw w⟩), but the VOWEL orthography is the obstacle: Marshallese has only four vowel phonemes whose front/back quality is allophonic, conditioned by the secondary articulation of the surrounding consonants, and the spelling records the surface quality rather than the phoneme. A correct spec therefore needs the consonant-conditioned vowel rules, not a flat grapheme map, and that research is not done here.
